description_long: This clock tower is a tall, rectangular structure constructed primarily from tan and brown stone blocks, giving it a robust and historic appearance. The upper section features a crenellated parapet with battlement-style notches, reminiscent of medieval fortifications. Below the parapet, there is a prominent arched opening on each visible side, flanked by decorative blue-tiled columns, suggesting a viewing or bell chamber. The stonework is detailed with horizontal banding and subtle textural variations, adding depth and realism to the facade. The base is slightly wider, providing stability and a sense of groundedness. No visible clock faces or timekeeping elements are present on any side of the tower.
description: A tall, tan stone clock tower with a crenellated roof, arched openings, and blue-tiled decorative columns, but no visible clock faces.
description_view_0: Front view showing the arched opening, blue-tiled columns, and detailed stonework beneath the crenellated parapet.
description_view_1: Side view nearly identical to the front, with the same arched opening and blue-tiled columns visible.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with the arched opening and blue-tiled columns, but no additional features.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the arched opening and blue-tiled columns, consistent with other sides.
